$comment: VENDORED COPY for the WS-A contract test in claude-copilot/tools/cc. Source of truth is copilot-control-tower/docs/01-architecture/schemas/projects.schema.json — update this copy from there, not the reverse.
title: copilot projects --json
description: Component Sync (docs/80-initiatives/02-component-sync/) machine-wide fan-out verbs. Two report shapes, distinguished structurally (never by an explicit discriminant field, since neither the freshness sweep nor the fan-out roll-up needs one to be unambiguous): the read-only all-projects freshness sweep (`projects`+`global`) and the write-side fan-out roll-up (`summary`+`results`). A single project's own `cc materialize --project <path> --json` result reuses update.schema.json's shape (plus its additive `path` property) rather than a third shape defined here -- see that schema's `path` property description.

total.description: Number of projects actually folded into `projects` below (a project skipped by the sweep's fail-open per-project guard is never counted).

projects.description: One entry per lock-manifested project discovered on the machine, PROJECT_SCOPED_PRODUCTS components only (claude, codex) -- global-once components are reported once in `global` below, never per project.

path.description: Absolute path to the project's own repo root.

stale.description: True if ANY tracked component is definitely outdated; null if any component's staleness is unknown (no latest version known for that product) and none is definitely outdated; false if every tracked component is known-current (including the vacuous case of zero tracked components). Never coerced -- same honesty rule as freshness.schema.json's `stale`.

current.description: The version recorded in this project's own tracked lock manifest. Null when the component's manifest entry has no recorded version.

latest.description: The latest published version for this product, as supplied to the sweep. Null when unknown (never fabricated).

stale.description: Null unless BOTH `current` and `latest` are known -- never coerced to false.

held.description: True when this component is stale AND at least one of its framework-owned paths currently sits inside a dirty git working tree in this project -- a preview of the hold a materialize run would report (ADR-002), computed read-only. Always false when `stale` is not true.

global.description: One deduped entry per GLOBAL_ONCE_PRODUCTS product (knowledge, cli) actually recorded by at least one discovered project's manifest -- reported once at machine scope, never once per project.

triggered_by.description: Provenance of this fan-out run, per the initiative README's Arc 4 mapping.

updated.description: Count of (project, component) pairs materialized (`result: applied`) this run -- powers the 'across N of your projects' line.

held.description: Count held for dirty WIP (`result: held`) -- 'waiting on your unsaved changes'.

failed.description: Count that neither applied nor held -- folds in both `blocked` (e.g. unverified target) and `offline` (unreachable source content) outcomes; each result item's own `result`/`report.result` field still distinguishes which.

results.description: Per-(project, component) outcome. A project-level read failure (manifest unreadable) has `component: null` and no `report`; every successful attempt carries the full per-project materialize `report` (update.schema.json shape + `path`).

result.description: Present ONLY for the two outcomes reported without a full per-project report: an honest up-to-date skip (nothing stale), or a project-level manifest-read failure.

reason.description: Present alongside `result: blocked` for a project-level read failure or an unexpected per-component error.

report.description: Present for every attempted materialize (applied/held/blocked/offline outcomes) -- the SAME per-project report `cc materialize --project` returns standalone.
